name: Georgia Peach Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the Crust: In a large bowl, combine the flour and salt. Using a pastry blender or two knives, cut in the butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Sprinkle with 5 to 6 tablespoons cold water, 1 tablespoon at a time, tossing with a fork to blend lightly but evenly. When the mixture forms a ball, gently knead 2 or 3 times. Divide the dough into half. On a lightly floured surface, roll out one half of the dough to a 12-inch circle and transfer it to a 9-inch pie plate. Trim, leaving a 1-inch overhang. Roll out the remaining half of the dough to a 12-inch circle and cut into ten strips, each about 1 inch wide. Set aside.
2. Heat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
3. Prepare the Filling: In a large bowl, combine the peaches, sugar, flour, lemon juice, and grated peel. Toss well. Pour the filling into the prepared crust. Arrange the reserved strips of dough in a lattice pattern over the filling, trimming them to match the lower edge of the crust overhang. Dampen the overhang lightly with water, fold up the edges, and seal. Flute the edge decoratively. Brush the lattice strips and edge of the crust with milk.
4. Bake for 50 minutes until bubbling and the crust is golden brown. Makes 10 servings.
